verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein of the immunoglobulin superfamily expressed in sperm. In mature capacitated/acrosome-reacted spermatozoa, IZUMO1 translocates to the sperm surface (outer leaflet) where it is essential for sperm-egg fusion, binding its receptor JUNO on the egg. Prior to acrosome reaction, IZUMO1 is sequestered in the acrosomal membrane and is not surface-exposed. This activation-state-dependent redistribution to the outer leaflet in a specific cell type (acrosome-reacted sperm) places it in contextual/tissue_restricted_surface territory — it has its own TM anchor but surface display is restricted to a narrow germline/reproductive lineage and a specific activation state.
